channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Authorities Investigate Appearance of Pro-Pakistan Poster and Video in Doda, J-K
item.description: In Doda district of Jammu and Kashmir, a pro-Pakistan poster and video have recently surfaced, prompting local authorities to initiate a formal investigation. The material, which has raised concerns a...

item.title: Man Pleads Guilty in 25-Year-Old Murder Case of Run-DMC's Jam Master Jay
item.description: A suspect has officially pleaded guilty in connection with the murder of Jam Master Jay, the legendary DJ of the iconic hip-hop group Run-DMC, more than 25 years after the killing took place. The murd...

item.title: Suspected Cerebrospinal Meningitis Outbreak Claims Lives of Mostly Children in Sokoto Village
item.description: A suspected outbreak of cerebrospinal meningitis (CSM) dey cause serious panic for Kurawa village wey dey inside Sabon Birni Local Government Area of Sokoto State. According to the village head, Mudas...

item.title: Report Argues Against Public Bank Financing for Nuclear Power Due to Economic Risks
item.description: A new report titled 'Debt, Delays, Dependencies' published in April 2026 by Urgewald and Ecodefense argues strongly against public banks and development institutions like the World Bank and Asian Deve...

item.title: India’s S400 Air Defence System: Initial Deployment and Next Phase
item.description: The S400 air defence system, previously deployed under the codename Operation Sindoor Star, continues to play a crucial role in India’s strategic defence planning. The system, which is among the most ...

item.title: Testing Adidas’ record-breaking super shoes: performance gains and practical limits
item.description: The article explores the experience of testing Adidas’ Adizero Adios Pro Evo 3, a new generation of ‘super shoes’ designed to enhance running performance and associated with recent marathon world reco...

item.title: Overview of Africa's Long-Serving Presidents and Monarchs
item.description: For Africa, some leaders don dey for power for long, sometimes more than 40 years. People like Teodoro Obiang Nguema Mbasogo for Equatorial Guinea and Paul Biya for Cameroon don hold their offices for...

item.title: 89-Year-Old Man Detained Following Shotgun Incident in Athens Injuring Multiple People
item.description: Authorities in Athens, Greece, have taken an 89-year-old man into custody after a reported incident involving a shotgun that resulted in injuries to at least four individuals. According to local law e...

item.title: Limpopo Premier Criticises Officials Over Renal Unit Construction Delays at Letaba Hospital
item.description: During an oversight visit to Letaba Hospital on Tuesday, Limpopo Premier Phophi Ramathuba expressed her frustration at senior health officials over delays in completing the hospital’s renal dialysis u...

item.title: IGAD Calls for Coordinated Mediation as Horn of Africa Faces Complex Conflicts
item.description: The Horn of Africa is currently facing a critical juncture with conflicts becoming more interconnected and influenced by external powers, according to Workneh Gebeyehu, Executive Secretary of IGAD. Sp...

item.title: Rybakina Loses to Potapova, Sabalenka Beats Osaka to Reach Madrid Open Quarter-Finals
item.description: For the Madrid Open on Monday, we see some major shocks as Kazakhstan's Elena Rybakina, wey be world number two, lose to Anastasia Potapova 7-6 (10/8), 6-4 for fourth round. Potapova, wey dey rank 56t...

item.title: Giordano Vaccaro, Winnipeg native, expected to be top pick in CFL draft
item.description: Giordano Vaccaro, a talented athlete from Winnipeg, is widely expected to be the first overall pick in the upcoming Canadian Football League (CFL) draft. Vaccaro's performance at the collegiate level ...

item.title: BIR initiates formal tax audits on Vice President Sara Duterte, her husband, and associated businesses
item.description: The Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) has formally started audit investigations into the tax records of Vice President Sara Duterte, her husband Mans Carpio, and nine of their associated businesses. Th...

item.title: Kenya's President Clarifies Remarks on English Proficiency in Africa
item.description: Kenya's President William Ruto has clarified remarks he made about Nigerians' English proficiency, following criticism from Nigerian citizens and social media debate. Earlier, during a speech to Kenya...

item.title: Kenny Loggins Discusses Retirement and Career in Toronto Documentary Premiere
item.description: Kenny Loggins, the renowned singer-songwriter best known for hits like 'Danger Zone' and 'Footloose,' recently premiered a documentary in Toronto that explores his life, career, and ongoing reflection...

item.title: Justice must be fair in probe of post-election violence, says Chande
item.description: The chairperson of Tanzania’s commission of inquiry into the violence linked to the 2025 General Election, Retired Chief Justice Mohamed Othman Chande, has emphasised the importance of fair and lawful...

item.title: Court Witness Details $6.23 Million Cash Withdrawal Under Former CBN Governor Emefiele
item.description: For Abuja, court matter dey hot as witness don show how $6.23 million cash waka commot from Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) vault during Godwin Emefiele time as governor. Eloho Okpoziakpo wey lead inves...

item.title: Young walrus Magnus explores Scottish coastline, delighting local communities
item.description: A young walrus named Magnus has been travelling along the Scottish coast, drawing considerable public attention. First sighted on the Orkney island of Stronsay earlier this month, he has since appeare...

item.title: Sophy Rickett’s Photographic Exploration of Women in Public Spaces, 1995
item.description: In 1995, British artist Sophie Rickett created a provocative photographic series titled 'Pissing Women', capturing women urinating while standing in public spaces across London. Inspired by her experi...

item.title: Pastor Corey Brooks Calls for Ending Excuses and Dependency in Chicago's South Side
item.description: In this opinion piece, Pastor Corey Brooks, founder of Project H.O.O.D. and known as the Rooftop Pastor, reflects on his recent Walk Across America and his return to Chicago's South Side. He observes ...
